 # Rust cheatsheet[m
[32m+[m
 Use Rust postgres-openssl to connect, insert, manage, query and stream from Materialize.[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m
 Materialize is **wire-compatible** with PostgreSQL, which means that Rust applications can use common PostgreSQL clients to interact with Materialize. In this guide, we'll use the [&#96;postgres-openssl&#96;](https://docs.rs/postgres-openssl/latest/postgres_openssl/) crate (the TLS support for [&#96;tokio-postgres&#96;](https://crates.io/crates/tokio-postgres) via &#96;openssl&#96;) to connect to Materialize and issue SQL commands.[m
 [m
 ## Connect[m
[33mdiff --git a/doc/user/static/markdown-docs/integrations/connection-pooling/index.md b/doc/user/static/markdown-docs/integrations/connection-pooling/index.md[m
[33mindex 690d664ae6..c948635a61 100644[m
[33m--- a/doc/user/static/markdown-docs/integrations/connection-pooling/index.md[m
[33m+++ b/doc/user/static/markdown-docs/integrations/connection-pooling/index.md[m
[35m@@ -1,5 +1,9 @@[m
 # Connection Pooling[m
[32m+[m
 How to use connection pooling with Materialize[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m
 Because Materialize is wire-compatible with PostgreSQL, you can use any[m
 PostgreSQL connection pooler with Materialize. In this guide, we’ll cover how to[m
 use connection pooling with Materialize, alongside the common tool PgBouncer.[m
[35m@@ -43,6 +47,7 @@[m [mSELECT rolname, rolpassword FROM pg_authid WHERE rolname = 'your_role_name';[m
 &#96;&#96;&#96;[m
 [m
 &gt; **Note:** You must be a superuser to access the &#96;pg_authid&#96; table.[m
[32m+[m[32m&gt;[m
 [m
 [m
 Once you have the SCRAM secret, add it to the userlist file in the following format:[m
[33mdiff --git a/doc/user/static/markdown-docs/integrations/fdw/index.md b/doc/user/static/markdown-docs/integrations/fdw/index.md[m
[33mindex 5d23bb5b0b..d897fed363 100644[m
[33m--- a/doc/user/static/markdown-docs/integrations/fdw/index.md[m
[33m+++ b/doc/user/static/markdown-docs/integrations/fdw/index.md[m
[35m@@ -1,5 +1,9 @@[m
 # Foreign data wrapper (FDW)[m
[32m+[m
 Use FDW to access Materialize[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m
 Materialize can be used as a remote server in a PostgreSQL foreign data wrapper[m
 (FDW). This allows you to query any object in Materialize as foreign tables from[m
 a PostgreSQL-compatible database. These objects appear as part of the local[m
[35m@@ -8,85 +12,80 @@[m [mrequiring changes to application logic or tooling.[m
 [m
 ## Prerequisite[m
 [m
[31m-1. In Materialize, create a dedicated service account &#96;fdw_svc_account&#96; as an[m
[31m-   **Organization Member**. For details on setting up a service account, see[m
[31m-   [Create a service[m
[31m-   account](https://materialize.com/docs/manage/users-service-accounts/create-service-accounts/)[m
[31m-[m
[31m-   &gt; **Tip:** Per the linked instructions, be sure you connect at least once with the new[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;ol&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;p&gt;In Materialize, create a dedicated service account &lt;code&gt;fdw_svc_account&lt;/code&gt; as an[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;strong&gt;Organization Member&lt;/strong&gt;. For details on setting up a service account, see[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;a href="https://materialize.com/docs/manage/users-service-accounts/create-service-accounts/" &gt;Create a service[m
[32m+[m[32maccount&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/p&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&gt; **Tip:** Per the linked instructions, be sure you connect at least once with the new[m
 &gt;    service account to finish creating the new account. You will also need the[m
 &gt;    connection details (host, port, password) when setting up the foreign server[m
 &gt;    and user mappings in PostgreSQL.[m
[32m+[m[32m&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&gt;[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;p&gt;After you have connected at least once with the new service account to finish[m
[32m+[m[32mthe new account creation, modify the &lt;code&gt;fdw_svc_account&lt;/code&gt; role:&lt;/p&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;ol&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;p&gt;Set the default cluster to the name of your serving cluster:&lt;/p&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;div class="highlight"&gt;&lt;pre tabindex="0" class="chroma"&gt;&lt;code class="language-mzsql" data-lang="mzsql"&gt;&lt;span class="line"&gt;&lt;span class="cl"&gt;&lt;span class="k"&gt;ALTER&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="k"&gt;ROLE&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="n"&gt;fdw_svc_account&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="k"&gt;SET&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="k"&gt;CLUSTER&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="o"&gt;=&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="o"&gt;&amp;lt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="n"&gt;serving_cluster&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="o"&gt;&amp;gt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="p"&gt;;&lt;/span&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/code&gt;&lt;/pre&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;/li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;p&gt;&lt;a href="/sql/grant-privilege/" &gt;Grant &lt;code&gt;USAGE&lt;/code&gt; privileges&lt;/a&gt; on the serving cluster,[m
[32m+[m[32mand the database and schema of your views and materialized views.&lt;/p&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;div class="highlight"&gt;&lt;pre tabindex="0" class="chroma"&gt;&lt;code class="language-mzsql" data-lang="mzsql"&gt;&lt;span class="line"&gt;&lt;span class="cl"&gt;&lt;span class="k"&gt;GRANT&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="k"&gt;USAGE&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="k"&gt;ON&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="k"&gt;CLUSTER&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="o"&gt;&amp;lt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="n"&gt;serving_cluster&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="o"&gt;&amp;gt;&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="k"&gt;TO&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="n"&gt;fdw_svc_account&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="p"&gt;;&lt;/span&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="line"&gt;&lt;span class="cl"&gt;&lt;span class="k"&gt;GRANT&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="k"&gt;USAGE&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="k"&gt;ON&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="k"&gt;DATABASE&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="o"&gt;&amp;lt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="n"&gt;db_name&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="o"&gt;&amp;gt;&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="k"&gt;TO&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="n"&gt;fdw_svc_account&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="p"&gt;;&lt;/span&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="line"&gt;&lt;span class="cl"&gt;&lt;span class="k"&gt;GRANT&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="k"&gt;USAGE&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="k"&gt;ON&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="k"&gt;SCHEMA&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="o"&gt;&amp;lt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="n"&gt;db_name&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="mf"&gt;.&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="n"&gt;schema_name&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="o"&gt;&amp;gt;&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="k"&gt;TO&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="n"&gt;fdw_svc_account&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="p"&gt;;&lt;/span&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/code&gt;&lt;/pre&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;/li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;p&gt;&lt;a href="/sql/grant-privilege/" &gt;Grant &lt;code&gt;SELECT&lt;/code&gt; privileges&lt;/a&gt; to the various[m
[32m+[m[32mview(s)/materialized view(s):&lt;/p&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;div class="highlight"&gt;&lt;pre tabindex="0" class="chroma"&gt;&lt;code class="language-mzsql" data-lang="mzsql"&gt;&lt;span class="line"&gt;&lt;span class="cl"&gt;&lt;span class="k"&gt;GRANT&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="k"&gt;SELECT&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="k"&gt;ON&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="o"&gt;&amp;lt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="n"&gt;db_name&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="mf"&gt;.&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="n"&gt;schema_name&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="mf"&gt;.&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="n"&gt;view_name&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="o"&gt;&amp;gt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="p"&gt;,&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="o"&gt;&amp;lt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="mf"&gt;...&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="o"&gt;&amp;gt;&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="k"&gt;TO&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="n"&gt;fdw_svc_account&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="p"&gt;;&lt;/span&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/code&gt;&lt;/pre&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;/li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/ol&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/ol&gt;[m
 [m
 [m
[31m-1. After you have connected at least once with the new service account to finish[m
[31m-   the new account creation, modify the &#96;fdw_svc_account&#96; role:[m
[31m-[m
[31m-   1. Set the default cluster to the name of your serving cluster:[m
[31m-[m
[31m-      &#96;&#96;&#96;mzsql[m
[31m-      ALTER ROLE fdw_svc_account SET CLUSTER = &lt;serving_cluster&gt;;[m
[31m-      &#96;&#96;&#96;[m
[31m-[m
[31m-   1. [Grant &#96;USAGE&#96; privileges](/sql/grant-privilege/) on the serving cluster,[m
[31m-      and the database and schema of your views and materialized views.[m
[31m-[m
[31m-      &#96;&#96;&#96;mzsql[m
[31m-      GRANT USAGE ON CLUSTER &lt;serving_cluster&gt; TO fdw_svc_account;[m
[31m-      GRANT USAGE ON DATABASE &lt;db_name&gt; TO fdw_svc_account;[m
[31m-      GRANT USAGE ON SCHEMA &lt;db_name.schema_name&gt; TO fdw_svc_account;[m
[31m-      &#96;&#96;&#96;[m
[31m-[m
[31m-   1. [Grant &#96;SELECT&#96; privileges](/sql/grant-privilege/) to the various[m
[31m-      view(s)/materialized view(s):[m
[31m-[m
[31m-      &#96;&#96;&#96;mzsql[m
[31m-      GRANT SELECT ON &lt;db_name.schema_name.view_name&gt;, &lt;...&gt; TO fdw_svc_account;[m
[31m-      &#96;&#96;&#96;[m
[31m-[m
 ## Setup FDW in PostgreSQL[m
 [m
[31m-**In your PostgreSQL instance**:[m
[31m-[m
[31m-1. If not installed, create a &#96;postgres_fdw&#96; extension in your database:[m
[31m-[m
[31m-   &#96;&#96;&#96;mzsql[m
[31m-   CREATE EXTENSION postgres_fdw;[m
[31m-   &#96;&#96;&#96;[m
[31m-[m
[31m-1. Create a foreign server to your Materialize, substitute your [Materialize[m
[31m-   connection details](/console/connect/).[m
[31m-[m
[31m-   &#96;&#96;&#96;mzsql[m
[31m-   CREATE SERVER remote_mz_server[m
[31m-      FOREIGN DATA WRAPPER postgres_fdw[m
[31m-      OPTIONS (host '&lt;host&gt;', dbname '&lt;db_name&gt;', port '6875');[m
[31m-   &#96;&#96;&#96;[m
[31m-[m
[31m-1. Create a user mapping between your PostgreSQL user and the Materialize[m
[31m-   &#96;fdw_svc_account&#96;:[m
[31m-[m
[31m-   &#96;&#96;&#96;mzsql[m
[31m-   CREATE USER MAPPING FOR &lt;postgres_user&gt;[m
[31m-      SERVER remote_mz_server[m
[31m-      OPTIONS (user 'fdw_svc_account', password '&lt;service_account_password&gt;');[m
[31m-   &#96;&#96;&#96;[m
[31m-[m
[31m-1. For each view/materialized view you want to access, create the foreign table[m
[31m-   mapping (you can use the [data explorer](/console/data/) to get the column[m
[31m-   detials)[m
[31m-[m
[31m-   &#96;&#96;&#96;mzsql[m
[31m-   CREATE FOREIGN TABLE &lt;local_view_name_in_postgres&gt; ([m
[31m-            &lt;column&gt; &lt;type&gt;,[m
[31m-            ...[m
[31m-        )[m
[31m-   SERVER remote_mz_server[m
[31m-   OPTIONS (schema_name '&lt;schema&gt;', table_name '&lt;view_name_in_Materialize&gt;');[m
[31m-   &#96;&#96;&#96;[m
[31m-[m
[31m-1. Once created, you can select from within PostgreSQL:[m
[31m-[m
[31m-   &#96;&#96;&#96;mzsql[m
[31m-   SELECT * from &lt;local_view_name_in_postgres&gt;;[m
[31m-   &#96;&#96;&#96;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;p&gt;&lt;strong&gt;In your PostgreSQL instance&lt;/strong&gt;:&lt;/p&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;ol&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;p&gt;If not installed, create a &lt;code&gt;postgres_fdw&lt;/code&gt; extension in your database:&lt;/p&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;div class="highlight"&gt;&lt;pre tabindex="0" class="chroma"&gt;&lt;code class="language-mzsql" data-lang="mzsql"&gt;&lt;span class="line"&gt;&lt;span class="cl"&gt;&lt;span class="k"&gt;CREATE&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="n"&gt;EXTENSION&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="n"&gt;postgres_fdw&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="p"&gt;;&lt;/span&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/code&gt;&lt;/pre&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;/li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;p&gt;Create a foreign server to your Materialize, substitute your &lt;a href="/console/connect/" &gt;Materialize[m
[32m+[m[32mconnection details&lt;/a&gt;.&lt;/p&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;div class="highlight"&gt;&lt;pre tabindex="0" class="chroma"&gt;&lt;code class="language-mzsql" data-lang="mzsql"&gt;&lt;span class="line"&gt;&lt;span class="cl"&gt;&lt;span class="k"&gt;CREATE&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="n"&gt;SERVER&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="n"&gt;remote_mz_server&lt;/span&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="line"&gt;&lt;span class="cl"&gt;   &lt;span class="k"&gt;FOREIGN&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="n"&gt;DATA&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="n"&gt;WRAPPER&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="n"&gt;postgres_fdw&lt;/span&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="line"&gt;&lt;span class="cl"&gt;   &lt;span class="k"&gt;OPTIONS&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="p"&gt;(&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="k"&gt;host&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="s1"&gt;&amp;#39;&amp;lt;host&amp;gt;&amp;#39;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="p"&gt;,&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="n"&gt;dbname&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="s1"&gt;&amp;#39;&amp;lt;db_name&amp;gt;&amp;#39;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="p"&gt;,&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="k"&gt;port&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="s1"&gt;&amp;#39;6875&amp;#39;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="p"&gt;);&lt;/span&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/code&gt;&lt;/pre&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;/li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;p&gt;Create a user mapping between your PostgreSQL user and the Materialize[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;code&gt;fdw_svc_account&lt;/code&gt;:&lt;/p&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;div class="highlight"&gt;&lt;pre tabindex="0" class="chroma"&gt;&lt;code class="language-mzsql" data-lang="mzsql"&gt;&lt;span class="line"&gt;&lt;span class="cl"&gt;&lt;span class="k"&gt;CREATE&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="k"&gt;USER&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="n"&gt;MAPPING&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="k"&gt;FOR&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="o"&gt;&amp;lt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="n"&gt;postgres_user&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="o"&gt;&amp;gt;&lt;/span&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="line"&gt;&lt;span class="cl"&gt;   &lt;span class="n"&gt;SERVER&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="n"&gt;remote_mz_server&lt;/span&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="line"&gt;&lt;span class="cl"&gt;   &lt;span class="k"&gt;OPTIONS&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="p"&gt;(&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="k"&gt;user&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="s1"&gt;&amp;#39;fdw_svc_account&amp;#39;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="p"&gt;,&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="k"&gt;password&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="s1"&gt;&amp;#39;&amp;lt;service_account_password&amp;gt;&amp;#39;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="p"&gt;);&lt;/span&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/code&gt;&lt;/pre&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;/li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;p&gt;For each view/materialized view you want to access, create the foreign table[m
[32m+[m[32mmapping (you can use the &lt;a href="/console/data/" &gt;data explorer&lt;/a&gt; to get the column[m
[32m+[m[32mdetials)&lt;/p&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;div class="highlight"&gt;&lt;pre tabindex="0" class="chroma"&gt;&lt;code class="language-mzsql" data-lang="mzsql"&gt;&lt;span class="line"&gt;&lt;span class="cl"&gt;&lt;span class="k"&gt;CREATE&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="k"&gt;FOREIGN&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="k"&gt;TABLE&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="o"&gt;&amp;lt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="n"&gt;local_view_name_in_postgres&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="o"&gt;&amp;gt;&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="p"&gt;(&lt;/span&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="line"&gt;&lt;span class="cl"&gt;         &lt;span class="o"&gt;&amp;lt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="k"&gt;column&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="o"&gt;&amp;gt;&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="o"&gt;&amp;lt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="k"&gt;type&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="o"&gt;&amp;gt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="p"&gt;,&lt;/span&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="line"&gt;&lt;span class="cl"&gt;         &lt;span class="mf"&gt;...&lt;/span&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="line"&gt;&lt;span class="cl"&gt;     &lt;span class="p"&gt;)&lt;/span&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="line"&gt;&lt;span class="cl"&gt;&lt;span class="n"&gt;SERVER&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="n"&gt;remote_mz_server&lt;/span&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="line"&gt;&lt;span class="cl"&gt;&lt;span class="k"&gt;OPTIONS&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="p"&gt;(&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="n"&gt;schema_name&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="s1"&gt;&amp;#39;&amp;lt;schema&amp;gt;&amp;#39;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="p"&gt;,&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="n"&gt;table_name&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="s1"&gt;&amp;#39;&amp;lt;view_name_in_Materialize&amp;gt;&amp;#39;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="p"&gt;);&lt;/span&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/code&gt;&lt;/pre&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;/li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;p&gt;Once created, you can select from within PostgreSQL:&lt;/p&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;div class="highlight"&gt;&lt;pre tabindex="0" class="chroma"&gt;&lt;code class="language-mzsql" data-lang="mzsql"&gt;&lt;span class="line"&gt;&lt;span class="cl"&gt;&lt;span class="k"&gt;SELECT&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="o"&gt;*&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="k"&gt;from&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="o"&gt;&amp;lt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="n"&gt;local_view_name_in_postgres&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="o"&gt;&amp;gt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="p"&gt;;&lt;/span&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/code&gt;&lt;/pre&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;/li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/ol&gt;[m
[33mdiff --git a/doc/user/static/markdown-docs/integrations/http-api/index.md b/doc/user/static/markdown-docs/integrations/http-api/index.md[m
[33mindex 2f5240ff6c..cf777fc6f7 100644[m
[33m--- a/doc/user/static/markdown-docs/integrations/http-api/index.md[m
[33m+++ b/doc/user/static/markdown-docs/integrations/http-api/index.md[m
[35m@@ -1,5 +1,9 @@[m
 # Connect to Materialize via HTTP[m
[32m+[m
 How to use Materialize via HTTP[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m
 You can access Materialize through its "session-less" HTTP API endpoint:[m
 [m
 &#96;&#96;&#96;bash[m
[33mdiff --git a/doc/user/static/markdown-docs/integrations/index.md b/doc/user/static/markdown-docs/integrations/index.md[m
[33mindex d2fe56e720..1daca735b1 100644[m
[33m--- a/doc/user/static/markdown-docs/integrations/index.md[m
[33m+++ b/doc/user/static/markdown-docs/integrations/index.md[m
[35m@@ -54,6 +54,7 @@[m [mlibraries to interact with Materialize and **create relations**, **execute[m
 queries** and **stream out results**.[m
 [m
 &gt; **Note:** Client libraries tend to run complex introspection queries that may use configuration settings, system tables or features not yet implemented in Materialize. This means that even if PostgreSQL is supported, it's **not guaranteed** that the same integration will work out-of-the-box.[m
[32m+[m[32m&gt;[m
 [m
 [m
 | Language | Tested drivers                                                  | Notes                                                 |[m
[35m@@ -788,6 +789,7 @@[m [mSELECT rolname, rolpassword FROM pg_authid WHERE rolname = 'your_role_name';[m
 &#96;&#96;&#96;[m
 [m
 &gt; **Note:** You must be a superuser to access the &#96;pg_authid&#96; table.[m
[32m+[m[32m&gt;[m
 [m
 [m
 Once you have the SCRAM secret, add it to the userlist file in the following format:[m
[35m@@ -853,88 +855,84 @@[m [mrequiring changes to application logic or tooling.[m
 [m
 ## Prerequisite[m
 [m
[31m-1. In Materialize, create a dedicated service account &#96;fdw_svc_account&#96; as an[m
[31m-   **Organization Member**. For details on setting up a service account, see[m
[31m-   [Create a service[m
[31m-   account](https://materialize.com/docs/manage/users-service-accounts/create-service-accounts/)[m
[31m-[m
[31m-   &gt; **Tip:** Per the linked instructions, be sure you connect at least once with the new[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;ol&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;p&gt;In Materialize, create a dedicated service account &lt;code&gt;fdw_svc_account&lt;/code&gt; as an[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;strong&gt;Organization Member&lt;/strong&gt;. For details on setting up a service account, see[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;a href="https://materialize.com/docs/manage/users-service-accounts/create-service-accounts/" &gt;Create a service[m
[32m+[m[32maccount&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/p&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&gt; **Tip:** Per the linked instructions, be sure you connect at least once with the new[m
 &gt;    service account to finish creating the new account. You will also need the[m
 &gt;    connection details (host, port, password) when setting up the foreign server[m
 &gt;    and user mappings in PostgreSQL.[m
[32m+[m[32m&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&gt;[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;p&gt;After you have connected at least once with the new service account to finish[m
[32m+[m[32mthe new account creation, modify the &lt;code&gt;fdw_svc_account&lt;/code&gt; role:&lt;/p&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;ol&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;p&gt;Set the default cluster to the name of your serving cluster:&lt;/p&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;div class="highlight"&gt;&lt;pre tabindex="0" class="chroma"&gt;&lt;code class="language-mzsql" data-lang="mzsql"&gt;&lt;span class="line"&gt;&lt;span class="cl"&gt;&lt;span class="k"&gt;ALTER&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="k"&gt;ROLE&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="n"&gt;fdw_svc_account&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="k"&gt;SET&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="k"&gt;CLUSTER&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="o"&gt;=&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="o"&gt;&amp;lt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="n"&gt;serving_cluster&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="o"&gt;&amp;gt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="p"&gt;;&lt;/span&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/code&gt;&lt;/pre&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;/li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;p&gt;&lt;a href="/sql/grant-privilege/" &gt;Grant &lt;code&gt;USAGE&lt;/code&gt; privileges&lt;/a&gt; on the serving cluster,[m
[32m+[m[32mand the database and schema of your views and materialized views.&lt;/p&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;div class="highlight"&gt;&lt;pre tabindex="0" class="chroma"&gt;&lt;code class="language-mzsql" data-lang="mzsql"&gt;&lt;span class="line"&gt;&lt;span class="cl"&gt;&lt;span class="k"&gt;GRANT&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="k"&gt;USAGE&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="k"&gt;ON&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="k"&gt;CLUSTER&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="o"&gt;&amp;lt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="n"&gt;serving_cluster&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="o"&gt;&amp;gt;&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="k"&gt;TO&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="n"&gt;fdw_svc_account&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="p"&gt;;&lt;/span&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="line"&gt;&lt;span class="cl"&gt;&lt;span class="k"&gt;GRANT&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="k"&gt;USAGE&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="k"&gt;ON&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="k"&gt;DATABASE&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="o"&gt;&amp;lt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="n"&gt;db_name&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="o"&gt;&amp;gt;&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="k"&gt;TO&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="n"&gt;fdw_svc_account&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="p"&gt;;&lt;/span&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="line"&gt;&lt;span class="cl"&gt;&lt;span class="k"&gt;GRANT&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="k"&gt;USAGE&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="k"&gt;ON&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="k"&gt;SCHEMA&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="o"&gt;&amp;lt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="n"&gt;db_name&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="mf"&gt;.&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="n"&gt;schema_name&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="o"&gt;&amp;gt;&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="k"&gt;TO&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="n"&gt;fdw_svc_account&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="p"&gt;;&lt;/span&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/code&gt;&lt;/pre&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;/li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;p&gt;&lt;a href="/sql/grant-privilege/" &gt;Grant &lt;code&gt;SELECT&lt;/code&gt; privileges&lt;/a&gt; to the various[m
[32m+[m[32mview(s)/materialized view(s):&lt;/p&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;div class="highlight"&gt;&lt;pre tabindex="0" class="chroma"&gt;&lt;code class="language-mzsql" data-lang="mzsql"&gt;&lt;span class="line"&gt;&lt;span class="cl"&gt;&lt;span class="k"&gt;GRANT&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="k"&gt;SELECT&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="k"&gt;ON&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="o"&gt;&amp;lt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="n"&gt;db_name&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="mf"&gt;.&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="n"&gt;schema_name&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="mf"&gt;.&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="n"&gt;view_name&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="o"&gt;&amp;gt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="p"&gt;,&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="o"&gt;&amp;lt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="mf"&gt;...&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="o"&gt;&amp;gt;&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="k"&gt;TO&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="n"&gt;fdw_svc_account&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="p"&gt;;&lt;/span&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/code&gt;&lt;/pre&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;/li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/ol&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/ol&gt;[m
 [m
 [m
[31m-1. After you have connected at least once with the new service account to finish[m
[31m-   the new account creation, modify the &#96;fdw_svc_account&#96; role:[m
[31m-[m
[31m-   1. Set the default cluster to the name of your serving cluster:[m
[31m-[m
[31m-      &#96;&#96;&#96;mzsql[m
[31m-      ALTER ROLE fdw_svc_account SET CLUSTER = &lt;serving_cluster&gt;;[m
[31m-      &#96;&#96;&#96;[m
[31m-[m
[31m-   1. [Grant &#96;USAGE&#96; privileges](/sql/grant-privilege/) on the serving cluster,[m
[31m-      and the database and schema of your views and materialized views.[m
[31m-[m
[31m-      &#96;&#96;&#96;mzsql[m
[31m-      GRANT USAGE ON CLUSTER &lt;serving_cluster&gt; TO fdw_svc_account;[m
[31m-      GRANT USAGE ON DATABASE &lt;db_name&gt; TO fdw_svc_account;[m
[31m-      GRANT USAGE ON SCHEMA &lt;db_name.schema_name&gt; TO fdw_svc_account;[m
[31m-      &#96;&#96;&#96;[m
[31m-[m
[31m-   1. [Grant &#96;SELECT&#96; privileges](/sql/grant-privilege/) to the various[m
[31m-      view(s)/materialized view(s):[m
[31m-[m
[31m-      &#96;&#96;&#96;mzsql[m
[31m-      GRANT SELECT ON &lt;db_name.schema_name.view_name&gt;, &lt;...&gt; TO fdw_svc_account;[m
[31m-      &#96;&#96;&#96;[m
[31m-[m
 ## Setup FDW in PostgreSQL[m
 [m
[31m-**In your PostgreSQL instance**:[m
[31m-[m
[31m-1. If not installed, create a &#96;postgres_fdw&#96; extension in your database:[m
[31m-[m
[31m-   &#96;&#96;&#96;mzsql[m
[31m-   CREATE EXTENSION postgres_fdw;[m
[31m-   &#96;&#96;&#96;[m
[31m-[m
[31m-1. Create a foreign server to your Materialize, substitute your [Materialize[m
[31m-   connection details](/console/connect/).[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;p&gt;&lt;strong&gt;In your PostgreSQL instance&lt;/strong&gt;:&lt;/p&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;ol&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;p&gt;If not installed, create a &lt;code&gt;postgres_fdw&lt;/code&gt; extension in your database:&lt;/p&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;div class="highlight"&gt;&lt;pre tabindex="0" class="chroma"&gt;&lt;code class="language-mzsql" data-lang="mzsql"&gt;&lt;span class="line"&gt;&lt;span class="cl"&gt;&lt;span class="k"&gt;CREATE&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="n"&gt;EXTENSION&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="n"&gt;postgres_fdw&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="p"&gt;;&lt;/span&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/code&gt;&lt;/pre&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;/li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;p&gt;Create a foreign server to your Materialize, substitute your &lt;a href="/console/connect/" &gt;Materialize[m
[32m+[m[32mconnection details&lt;/a&gt;.&lt;/p&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;div class="highlight"&gt;&lt;pre tabindex="0" class="chroma"&gt;&lt;code class="language-mzsql" data-lang="mzsql"&gt;&lt;span class="line"&gt;&lt;span class="cl"&gt;&lt;span class="k"&gt;CREATE&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="n"&gt;SERVER&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="n"&gt;remote_mz_server&lt;/span&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="line"&gt;&lt;span class="cl"&gt;   &lt;span class="k"&gt;FOREIGN&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="n"&gt;DATA&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="n"&gt;WRAPPER&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="n"&gt;postgres_fdw&lt;/span&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="line"&gt;&lt;span class="cl"&gt;   &lt;span class="k"&gt;OPTIONS&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="p"&gt;(&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="k"&gt;host&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="s1"&gt;&amp;#39;&amp;lt;host&amp;gt;&amp;#39;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="p"&gt;,&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="n"&gt;dbname&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="s1"&gt;&amp;#39;&amp;lt;db_name&amp;gt;&amp;#39;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="p"&gt;,&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="k"&gt;port&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="s1"&gt;&amp;#39;6875&amp;#39;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="p"&gt;);&lt;/span&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/code&gt;&lt;/pre&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;/li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;p&gt;Create a user mapping between your PostgreSQL user and the Materialize[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;code&gt;fdw_svc_account&lt;/code&gt;:&lt;/p&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;div class="highlight"&gt;&lt;pre tabindex="0" class="chroma"&gt;&lt;code class="language-mzsql" data-lang="mzsql"&gt;&lt;span class="line"&gt;&lt;span class="cl"&gt;&lt;span class="k"&gt;CREATE&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="k"&gt;USER&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="n"&gt;MAPPING&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="k"&gt;FOR&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="o"&gt;&amp;lt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="n"&gt;postgres_user&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="o"&gt;&amp;gt;&lt;/span&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="line"&gt;&lt;span class="cl"&gt;   &lt;span class="n"&gt;SERVER&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="n"&gt;remote_mz_server&lt;/span&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="line"&gt;&lt;span class="cl"&gt;   &lt;span class="k"&gt;OPTIONS&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="p"&gt;(&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="k"&gt;user&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="s1"&gt;&amp;#39;fdw_svc_account&amp;#39;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="p"&gt;,&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="k"&gt;password&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="s1"&gt;&amp;#39;&amp;lt;service_account_password&amp;gt;&amp;#39;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="p"&gt;);&lt;/span&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/code&gt;&lt;/pre&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;/li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;p&gt;For each view/materialized view you want to access, create the foreign table[m
[32m+[m[32mmapping (you can use the &lt;a href="/console/data/" &gt;data explorer&lt;/a&gt; to get the column[m
[32m+[m[32mdetials)&lt;/p&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;div class="highlight"&gt;&lt;pre tabindex="0" class="chroma"&gt;&lt;code class="language-mzsql" data-lang="mzsql"&gt;&lt;span class="line"&gt;&lt;span class="cl"&gt;&lt;span class="k"&gt;CREATE&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="k"&gt;FOREIGN&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="k"&gt;TABLE&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="o"&gt;&amp;lt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="n"&gt;local_view_name_in_postgres&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="o"&gt;&amp;gt;&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="p"&gt;(&lt;/span&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="line"&gt;&lt;span class="cl"&gt;         &lt;span class="o"&gt;&amp;lt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="k"&gt;column&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="o"&gt;&amp;gt;&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="o"&gt;&amp;lt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="k"&gt;type&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="o"&gt;&amp;gt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="p"&gt;,&lt;/span&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="line"&gt;&lt;span class="cl"&gt;         &lt;span class="mf"&gt;...&lt;/span&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="line"&gt;&lt;span class="cl"&gt;     &lt;span class="p"&gt;)&lt;/span&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="line"&gt;&lt;span class="cl"&gt;&lt;span class="n"&gt;SERVER&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="n"&gt;remote_mz_server&lt;/span&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="line"&gt;&lt;span class="cl"&gt;&lt;span class="k"&gt;OPTIONS&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="p"&gt;(&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="n"&gt;schema_name&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="s1"&gt;&amp;#39;&amp;lt;schema&amp;gt;&amp;#39;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="p"&gt;,&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="n"&gt;table_name&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="s1"&gt;&amp;#39;&amp;lt;view_name_in_Materialize&amp;gt;&amp;#39;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="p"&gt;);&lt;/span&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/code&gt;&lt;/pre&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;/li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;p&gt;Once created, you can select from within PostgreSQL:&lt;/p&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;div class="highlight"&gt;&lt;pre tabindex="0" class="chroma"&gt;&lt;code class="language-mzsql" data-lang="mzsql"&gt;&lt;span class="line"&gt;&lt;span class="cl"&gt;&lt;span class="k"&gt;SELECT&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="o"&gt;*&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="k"&gt;from&lt;/span&gt; &lt;span class="o"&gt;&amp;lt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="n"&gt;local_view_name_in_postgres&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="o"&gt;&amp;gt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;span class="p"&gt;;&lt;/span&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/code&gt;&lt;/pre&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;/li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/ol&gt;[m
 [m
[31m-   &#96;&#96;&#96;mzsql[m
[31m-   CREATE SERVER remote_mz_server[m
[31m-      FOREIGN DATA WRAPPER postgres_fdw[m
[31m-      OPTIONS (host '&lt;host&gt;', dbname '&lt;db_name&gt;', port '6875');[m
[31m-   &#96;&#96;&#96;[m
[31m-[m
[31m-1. Create a user mapping between your PostgreSQL user and the Materialize[m
[31m-   &#96;fdw_svc_account&#96;:[m
[31m-[m
[31m-   &#96;&#96;&#96;mzsql[m
[31m-   CREATE USER MAPPING FOR &lt;postgres_user&gt;[m
[31m-      SERVER remote_mz_server[m
[31m-      OPTIONS (user 'fdw_svc_account', password '&lt;service_account_password&gt;');[m
[31m-   &#96;&#96;&#96;[m
[31m-[m
[31m-1. For each view/materialized view you want to access, create the foreign table[m
[31m-   mapping (you can use the [data explorer](/console/data/) to get the column[m
[31m-   detials)[m
[31m-[m
[31m-   &#96;&#96;&#96;mzsql[m
[31m-   CREATE FOREIGN TABLE &lt;local_view_name_in_postgres&gt; ([m
[31m-            &lt;column&gt; &lt;type&gt;,[m
[31m-            ...[m
[31m-        )[m
[31m-   SERVER remote_mz_server[m
[31m-   OPTIONS (schema_name '&lt;schema&gt;', table_name '&lt;view_name_in_Materialize&gt;');[m
[31m-   &#96;&#96;&#96;[m
[31m-[m
[31m-1. Once created, you can select from within PostgreSQL:[m
[31m-[m
[31m-   &#96;&#96;&#96;mzsql[m
[31m-   SELECT * from &lt;local_view_name_in_postgres&gt;;[m
[31m-   &#96;&#96;&#96;[m
 [m
 [m
 ---[m
[35m@@ -1238,6 +1236,7 @@[m [muser. This password is auto-generated, and prefixed with &#96;mzp_&#96;.[m
 &gt; session**, but do not affect other sessions. To permanently change the default[m
 &gt; value of a configuration parameter for a specific user (i.e. role), use the[m
 &gt; [&#96;ALTER ROLE...SET&#96;](/sql/alter-role) command.[m
[32m+[m[32m&gt;[m
 [m
 [m
 You can pass runtime connection parameters (like &#96;cluster&#96;, &#96;isolation_level&#96;,[m
[35m@@ -1272,12 +1271,15 @@[m [mpsql \[m
 &gt; -- may not work as expected with Materialize because they rely on[m
 &gt; PostgreSQL-specific queries that use unsupported system functions (e.g.,[m
 &gt; &#96;age()&#96;) and system columns (e.g., &#96;xmin&#96;).[m
[32m+[m[32m&gt;[m
 &gt; As an alternative, you can [use the JDBC metadata[m
 &gt; introspector](https://www.jetbrains.com/help/datagrip/cannot-find-a-database-object-in-the-database-tree-view.html#temporarily-enable-introspection-with-jdbc-metadata).[m
 &gt; To use the JDBC metadata instrospector, from your data source properties, in the[m
 &gt; **Advanced** tab, select **Introspect using JDBC Metadata** from the **Expert[m
 &gt; options** list. For more information, see the [DataGrip[m
 &gt; documentation](https://www.jetbrains.com/help/datagrip/cannot-find-a-database-object-in-the-database-tree-view.html#temporarily-enable-introspection-with-jdbc-metadata).[m
[32m+[m[32m&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&gt;[m
 [m
 [m
 To connect to Materialize using [DataGrip](https://www.jetbrains.com/help/datagrip/connecting-to-a-database.html),[m
[35m@@ -1347,6 +1349,7 @@[m [mTo show system objects in the database explorer:[m
 [m
 &gt; **Note:** As we work on extending the coverage of &#96;pg_catalog&#96; in Materialize,[m
 &gt; some TablePlus features might not work as expected.[m
[32m+[m[32m&gt;[m
 [m
 [m
 To connect to Materialize using [TablePlus](https://tableplus.com/),[m
[35m@@ -1359,6 +1362,7 @@[m [mMaterialize console.[m
 ### &#96;psql&#96;[m
 [m
 &gt; **Warning:** Not all features of &#96;psql&#96; are supported by Materialize yet, including some backslash meta-commands.[m
[32m+[m[32m&gt;[m
 [m
 [m
 [m
[33mdiff --git a/doc/user/static/markdown-docs/integrations/llm/index.md b/doc/user/static/markdown-docs/integrations/llm/index.md[m
[33mindex 1d2d057ecf..c4f24d6744 100644[m
[33m--- a/doc/user/static/markdown-docs/integrations/llm/index.md[m
[33m+++ b/doc/user/static/markdown-docs/integrations/llm/index.md[m
[35m@@ -1,5 +1,9 @@[m
 # MCP Server[m
[32m+[m
 Learn how to integrate Materialize with Large Language Models (LLMs) using MCP[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m
 The [Model Context Protocol (MCP) Server for Materialize](https://materialize.com/blog/materialize-turns-views-into-tools-for-agents/) lets large language models (LLMs) call your indexed views as real-time tools.[m
 The MCP Server automatically turns any indexed view with a comment into a callable, typed interface that LLMs can use to fetch structured, up-to-date answers—directly from the database.[m
 [m

[32m+[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m
 If the [recommended three-tier[m
 architecture](/manage/operational-guidelines/#three-tier-architecture)[m
 is infeasible or unnecessary due to low volume or a **non**-production setup, a[m
[35m@@ -17,7 +21,7 @@[m [mServing cluster" &gt;&lt;/p&gt;[m
 | Tier | Description |[m
 | --- | --- |[m
 | &lt;strong&gt;Source cluster(s)&lt;/strong&gt; | &lt;p&gt;&lt;strong&gt;A dedicated cluster(s)&lt;/strong&gt; for &lt;a href="/concepts/sources/" &gt;sources&lt;/a&gt;.&lt;/p&gt; &lt;p&gt;In addition, for upsert sources:&lt;/p&gt; &lt;ul&gt; &lt;li&gt; &lt;p&gt;Consider separating upsert sources from your other sources. Upsert sources have higher resource requirements (since, for upsert sources, Materialize maintains each key and associated last value for the key as well as to perform deduplication). As such, if possible, use a separate source cluster for upsert sources.&lt;/p&gt; &lt;/li&gt; &lt;li&gt; &lt;p&gt;Consider using a larger cluster size during snapshotting for upsert sources. Once the snapshotting operation is complete, you can downsize the cluster to align with the steady-state ingestion.&lt;/p&gt; &lt;/li&gt; &lt;/ul&gt;  |[m
[31m-| &lt;strong&gt;Compute/Transform + Serving cluster&lt;/strong&gt; | &lt;p&gt;&lt;strong&gt;A dedicated cluster&lt;/strong&gt; for both compute/transformation and serving queries:&lt;/p&gt; &lt;ul&gt; &lt;li&gt; &lt;p&gt;&lt;a href="/concepts/views/#views" &gt;Views&lt;/a&gt; that define the transformations.&lt;/p&gt; &lt;/li&gt; &lt;li&gt; &lt;p&gt;Indexes on views to maintain up-to-date results in memory and serve queries.&lt;/p&gt; &lt;/li&gt; &lt;/ul&gt; &lt;p&gt;With a two-tier architecture, compute and queries compete for the same cluster resources.&lt;/p&gt; &gt; **Tip:** Except for when used with a [sink](/serve-results/sink/), &gt; [subscribe](/sql/subscribe/), or [temporal &gt; filters](/transform-data/patterns/temporal-filters/), avoid creating &gt; materialized views on a shared cluster used for both compute/transformat &gt; operations and serving queries. Use indexed views instead.    |[m
[32m+[m[32m| &lt;strong&gt;Compute/Transform + Serving cluster&lt;/strong&gt; | &lt;p&gt;&lt;strong&gt;A dedicated cluster&lt;/strong&gt; for both compute/transformation and serving queries:&lt;/p&gt; &lt;ul&gt; &lt;li&gt; &lt;p&gt;&lt;a href="/concepts/views/#views" &gt;Views&lt;/a&gt; that define the transformations.&lt;/p&gt; &lt;/li&gt; &lt;li&gt; &lt;p&gt;Indexes on views to maintain up-to-date results in memory and serve queries.&lt;/p&gt; &lt;/li&gt; &lt;/ul&gt; &lt;p&gt;With a two-tier architecture, compute and queries compete for the same cluster resources.&lt;/p&gt; &gt; **Tip:** Except for when used with a [sink](/serve-results/sink/), &gt; [subscribe](/sql/subscribe/), or [temporal &gt; filters](/transform-data/patterns/temporal-filters/), avoid creating &gt; materialized views on a shared cluster used for both compute/transformat &gt; operations and serving queries.  # Materialize Cloud DR characteristics[m
[32m+[m
 Reference on various failure modes and recovery characteristics of Materialize Cloud.[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m
 The following provides  various failure mode impact and recovery[m
 characteristics for Materialize.[m
 [m
[33mdiff --git a/doc/user/static/markdown-docs/manage/index.md b/doc/user/static/markdown-docs/manage/index.md[m
[33mindex d90c2ff8f1..9b3d78db17 100644[m
[33m--- a/doc/user/static/markdown-docs/manage/index.md[m
[33m+++ b/doc/user/static/markdown-docs/manage/index.md[m
[35m@@ -50,7 +50,7 @@[m [mServing cluster" &gt;&lt;/p&gt;[m
 | Tier | Description |[m
 | --- | --- |[m
 | &lt;strong&gt;Source cluster(s)&lt;/strong&gt; | &lt;p&gt;&lt;strong&gt;A dedicated cluster(s)&lt;/strong&gt; for &lt;a href="/concepts/sources/" &gt;sources&lt;/a&gt;.&lt;/p&gt; &lt;p&gt;In addition, for upsert sources:&lt;/p&gt; &lt;ul&gt; &lt;li&gt; &lt;p&gt;Consider separating upsert sources from your other sources. Upsert sources have higher resource requirements (since, for upsert sources, Materialize maintains each key and associated last value for the key as well as to perform deduplication). As such, if possible, use a separate source cluster for upsert sources.&lt;/p&gt; &lt;/li&gt; &lt;li&gt; &lt;p&gt;Consider using a larger cluster size during snapshotting for upsert sources. Once the snapshotting operation is complete, you can downsize the cluster to align with the steady-state ingestion.&lt;/p&gt; &lt;/li&gt; &lt;/ul&gt;  |[m
[31m-| &lt;strong&gt;Compute/Transform + Serving cluster&lt;/strong&gt; | &lt;p&gt;&lt;strong&gt;A dedicated cluster&lt;/strong&gt; for both compute/transformation and serving queries:&lt;/p&gt; &lt;ul&gt; &lt;li&gt; &lt;p&gt;&lt;a href="/concepts/views/#views" &gt;Views&lt;/a&gt; that define the transformations.&lt;/p&gt; &lt;/li&gt; &lt;li&gt; &lt;p&gt;Indexes on views to maintain up-to-date results in memory and serve queries.&lt;/p&gt; &lt;/li&gt; &lt;/ul&gt; &lt;p&gt;With a two-tier architecture, compute and queries compete for the same cluster resources.&lt;/p&gt; &gt; **Tip:** Except for when used with a [sink](/serve-results/sink/), &gt; [subscribe](/sql/subscribe/), or [temporal &gt; filters](/transform-data/patterns/temporal-filters/), avoid creating &gt; materialized views on a shared cluster used for both compute/transformat &gt; operations and serving queries. Use indexed views instead.    |[m
[32m+[m[32m| &lt;strong&gt;Compute/Transform + Serving cluster&lt;/strong&gt; | &lt;p&gt;&lt;strong&gt;A dedicated cluster&lt;/strong&gt; for both compute/transformation and serving queries:&lt;/p&gt; &lt;ul&gt; &lt;li&gt; &lt;p&gt;&lt;a href="/concepts/views/#views" &gt;Views&lt;/a&gt; that define the transformations.&lt;/p&gt; &lt;/li&gt; &lt;li&gt; &lt;p&gt;Indexes on views to maintain up-to-date results in memory and serve queries.&lt;/p&gt; &lt;/li&gt; &lt;/ul&gt; &lt;p&gt;With a two-tier architecture, compute and queries compete for the same cluster resources.&lt;/p&gt; &gt; **Tip:** Except for when used with a [sink](/serve-results/sink/), &gt; [subscribe](/sql/subscribe/), or [temporal &gt; filters](/transform-data/patterns/temporal-filters/), avoid creating &gt; materialized views on a shared cluster used for both compute/transformat &gt; operations and serving queries. Use indexed views instead. &gt;     |[m
 [m
 &lt;p&gt;Benefits of a two-tier architecture include:&lt;/p&gt;[m
 &lt;ul&gt;[m
[35m@@ -86,7 +86,7 @@[m [marchitecture" &gt;&lt;/p&gt;[m
 [m
 | Tier | Description |[m
 | --- | --- |[m
[31m-| &lt;strong&gt;All-in-one cluster&lt;/strong&gt; | &lt;p&gt;&lt;strong&gt;A cluster&lt;/strong&gt; for &lt;a href="/concepts/sources/" &gt;sources&lt;/a&gt;, compute/transformation and serving queries:&lt;/p&gt; &lt;ul&gt; &lt;li&gt; &lt;p&gt;Sources to ingest data.&lt;/p&gt; &lt;/li&gt; &lt;li&gt; &lt;p&gt;Views that define the transformations.&lt;/p&gt; &lt;/li&gt; &lt;li&gt; &lt;p&gt;Indexes on views to maintain up-to-date results in memory and serve queries.&lt;/p&gt; &lt;/li&gt; &lt;/ul&gt; &lt;p&gt;With a 1-tier single-cluster architecture, sources, compute, and queries compete for the same cluster resources.&lt;/p&gt; &gt; **Tip:** Except for when used with a [sink](/serve-results/sink/), &gt; [subscribe](/sql/subscribe/), or [temporal &gt; filters](/transform-data/patterns/temporal-filters/), avoid creating &gt; materialized views on a shared cluster used for both compute/transformat &gt; operations and serving queries. Use indexed views instead.    |[m
[32m+[m[32m| &lt;strong&gt;All-in-one cluster&lt;/strong&gt; | &lt;p&gt;&lt;strong&gt;A cluster&lt;/strong&gt; for &lt;a href="/concepts/sources/" &gt;sources&lt;/a&gt;, compute/transformation and serving queries:&lt;/p&gt; &lt;ul&gt; &lt;li&gt; &lt;p&gt;Sources to ingest data.&lt;/p&gt; &lt;/li&gt; &lt;li&gt; &lt;p&gt;Views that define the transformations.&lt;/p&gt; &lt;/li&gt; &lt;li&gt; &lt;p&gt;Indexes on views to maintain up-to-date results in memory and serve queries.&lt;/p&gt; &lt;/li&gt; &lt;/ul&gt; &lt;p&gt;With a 1-tier single-cluster architecture, sources, compute, and queries compete for the same cluster resources.&lt;/p&gt; &gt; **Tip:** Except for when used with a [sink](/serve-results/sink/), &gt; [subscribe](/sql/subscribe/), or [temporal &gt; filters](/transform-data/patterns/temporal-filters/), avoid creating &gt; materialized views on a shared cluster used for both compute/transformat &gt; operations and serving queries. Use indexed views instead. &gt;     |[m
 [m
 &lt;p&gt;&lt;strong&gt;Benefits of a one-tier architecture&lt;/strong&gt; include:&lt;/p&gt;[m
 &lt;ul&gt;[m

[35m@@ -301,7 +306,7 @@[m [marchitecture"&gt;&lt;/p&gt;[m
 | Tier | Description |[m
 | --- | --- |[m
 | &lt;strong&gt;Source cluster(s)&lt;/strong&gt; | &lt;p&gt;&lt;strong&gt;A dedicated cluster(s)&lt;/strong&gt; for &lt;a href="/concepts/sources/" &gt;sources&lt;/a&gt;.&lt;/p&gt; &lt;p&gt;In addition, for upsert sources:&lt;/p&gt; &lt;ul&gt; &lt;li&gt; &lt;p&gt;Consider separating upsert sources from your other sources. Upsert sources have higher resource requirements (since, for upsert sources, Materialize maintains each key and associated last value for the key as well as to perform deduplication). As such, if possible, use a separate source cluster for upsert sources.&lt;/p&gt; &lt;/li&gt; &lt;li&gt; &lt;p&gt;Consider using a larger cluster size during snapshotting for upsert sources. Once the snapshotting operation is complete, you can downsize the cluster to align with the steady-state ingestion.&lt;/p&gt; &lt;/li&gt; &lt;/ul&gt;  |[m
[31m-| &lt;strong&gt;Compute/Transform cluster(s)&lt;/strong&gt; | &lt;p&gt;&lt;strong&gt;A dedicated cluster(s)&lt;/strong&gt; for compute/transformation:&lt;/p&gt; &lt;ul&gt; &lt;li&gt; &lt;p&gt;&lt;a href="/concepts/views/#materialized-views" &gt;Materialized views&lt;/a&gt; to persist, in durable storage, the results that will be served. Results of materialized views are available across all clusters.&lt;/p&gt; &gt; **Tip:** If you are using &lt;strong&gt;stacked views&lt;/strong&gt; (i.e., views whose definition depends &gt;   on other views) to reduce SQL complexity, generally, only the topmost &gt;   view (i.e., the view whose results will be served) should be a &gt;   materialized view. The underlying views that do not serve results do not &gt;   need to be materialized.  &lt;/li&gt; &lt;li&gt; &lt;p&gt;Indexes, &lt;strong&gt;only as needed&lt;/strong&gt;, to make transformation fast (such as possibly &lt;a href="/transform-data/optimization/#optimize-multi-way-joins-with-delta-joins" &gt;indexes on join keys&lt;/a&gt;).&lt;/p&gt; &gt; **Tip:** From the compute/transformation clusters, do not create indexes on the &gt;   materialized views for the purposes of serving the view results. &gt;   Instead, use the [serving cluster(s)](#tier-serving-clusters) when &gt;   creating indexes to serve the results.  &lt;/li&gt; &lt;/ul&gt;  |[m
[32m+[m[32m| &lt;strong&gt;Compute/Transform cluster(s)&lt;/strong&gt; | &lt;p&gt;&lt;strong&gt;A dedicated cluster(s)&lt;/strong&gt; for compute/transformation:&lt;/p&gt; &lt;ul&gt; &lt;li&gt; &lt;p&gt;&lt;a href="/concepts/views/#materialized-views" &gt;Materialized views&lt;/a&gt; to persist, in durable storage, the results that will be served. Results of materialized views are available across all clusters.&lt;/p&gt; &gt; **Tip:** If you are using &lt;strong&gt;stacked views&lt;/strong&gt; (i.e., views whose definition depends &gt;   on other views) to reduce SQL complexity, generally, only the topmost &gt;   view (i.e., the view whose results will be served) should be a &gt;   materialized view. The underlying views that do not serve results do not &gt;   need to be materialized. &gt;     &lt;/li&gt; &lt;li&gt; &lt;p&gt;Indexes, &lt;strong&gt;only as needed&lt;/strong&gt;, to make transformation fast (such as possibly &lt;a href="/transform-data/optimization/#optimize-multi-way-joins-with-delta-joins" &gt;indexes on join keys&lt;/a&gt;).&lt;/p&gt; &gt; **Tip:** From the compute/transformation clusters, do not create indexes on the &gt;   materialized views for the purposes of serving the view results. &gt;   Instead, use the [serving cluster(s)](#tier-serving-clusters) when &gt;   creating indexes to serve the results. &gt;  &gt;     &lt;/li&gt; &lt;/ul&gt;  |[m
 | &lt;strong&gt;Serving cluster(s)&lt;/strong&gt; | &lt;a name="tier-serving-clusters"&gt;&lt;/a&gt; &lt;strong&gt;A dedicated cluster(s)&lt;/strong&gt; for serving queries, including &lt;a href="/concepts/indexes/" &gt;indexes&lt;/a&gt; on the materialized views. Indexes are local to the cluster in which they are created. |[m
 [m
 &lt;p&gt;Benefits of a three-tier architecture include:&lt;/p&gt;[m

 # Alerting[m
[32m+[m
 Alerting thresholds to use for monitoring.[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m
 After setting up a monitoring tool, it is important to configure alert rules. Alert rules send a notification when a metric surpasses a threshold. This will help you prevent operational incidents.[m
 [m
 This page describes which metrics and thresholds to build as a starting point. For more details on how to set up alert rules in Datadog or Grafana, refer to:[m

[33mindex caf65540c5..d1f7d6fa58 100644[m

[35m@@ -1,5 +1,9 @@[m
 # Operational guidelines[m
[32m+[m
 General guidelines for production[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m
 The following provides some general guidelines for production.[m
 [m
 ## Clusters[m
[35m@@ -20,7 +24,7 @@[m [marchitecture"&gt;&lt;/p&gt;[m
 | Tier | Description |[m
 | --- | --- |[m
 | &lt;strong&gt;Source cluster(s)&lt;/strong&gt; | &lt;p&gt;&lt;strong&gt;A dedicated cluster(s)&lt;/strong&gt; for &lt;a href="/concepts/sources/" &gt;sources&lt;/a&gt;.&lt;/p&gt; &lt;p&gt;In addition, for upsert sources:&lt;/p&gt; &lt;ul&gt; &lt;li&gt; &lt;p&gt;Consider separating upsert sources from your other sources. Upsert sources have higher resource requirements (since, for upsert sources, Materialize maintains each key and associated last value for the key as well as to perform deduplication). As such, if possible, use a separate source cluster for upsert sources.&lt;/p&gt; &lt;/li&gt; &lt;li&gt; &lt;p&gt;Consider using a larger cluster size during snapshotting for upsert sources. Once the snapshotting operation is complete, you can downsize the cluster to align with the steady-state ingestion.&lt;/p&gt; &lt;/li&gt; &lt;/ul&gt;  |[m
[31m-| &lt;strong&gt;Compute/Transform cluster(s)&lt;/strong&gt; | &lt;p&gt;&lt;strong&gt;A dedicated cluster(s)&lt;/strong&gt; for compute/transformation:&lt;/p&gt; &lt;ul&gt; &lt;li&gt; &lt;p&gt;&lt;a href="/concepts/views/#materialized-views" &gt;Materialized views&lt;/a&gt; to persist, in durable storage, the results that will be served. Results of materialized views are available across all clusters.&lt;/p&gt; &gt; **Tip:** If you are using &lt;strong&gt;stacked views&lt;/strong&gt; (i.e., views whose definition depends &gt;   on other views) to reduce SQL complexity, generally, only the topmost &gt;   view (i.e., the view whose results will be served) should be a &gt;   materialized view. The underlying views that do not serve results do not &gt;   need to be materialized.  &lt;/li&gt; &lt;li&gt; &lt;p&gt;Indexes, &lt;strong&gt;only as needed&lt;/strong&gt;, to make transformation fast (such as possibly &lt;a href="/transform-data/optimization/#optimize-multi-way-joins-with-delta-joins" &gt;indexes on join keys&lt;/a&gt;).&lt;/p&gt; &gt; **Tip:** From the compute/transformation clusters, do not create indexes on the &gt;   materialized views for the purposes of serving the view results. &gt;   Instead, use the [serving cluster(s)](#tier-serving-clusters) when &gt;   creating indexes to serve the results.  &lt;/li&gt; &lt;/ul&gt;  |[m
[32m+[m[32m| &lt;strong&gt;Compute/Transform cluster(s)&lt;/strong&gt; | &lt;p&gt;&lt;strong&gt;A dedicated cluster(s)&lt;/strong&gt; for compute/transformation:&lt;/p&gt; &lt;ul&gt; &lt;li&gt; &lt;p&gt;&lt;a href="/concepts/views/#materialized-views" &gt;Materialized views&lt;/a&gt; to persist, in durable storage, the results that will be served. Results of materialized views are available across all clusters.&lt;/p&gt; &gt; **Tip:** If you are using &lt;strong&gt;stacked views&lt;/strong&gt; (i.e., views whose definition depends &gt;   on other views) to reduce SQL complexity, generally, only the topmost &gt;   view (i.e., the view whose results will be served) should be a &gt;   materialized view. The underlying views that do not serve results do not &gt;   need to be materialized. &gt;     &lt;/li&gt; &lt;li&gt; &lt;p&gt;Indexes, &lt;strong&gt;only as needed&lt;/strong&gt;, to make transformation fast (such as possibly &lt;a href="/transform-data/optimization/#optimize-multi-way-joins-with-delta-joins" &gt;indexes on join keys&lt;/a&gt;).&lt;/p&gt; &gt; **Tip:** From the compute/transformation clusters, do not create indexes on the &gt;   materialized views for the purposes of serving the view results. &gt;   Instead, use the [serving cluster(s)](#tier-serving-clusters) when &gt;   creating indexes to serve the results. &gt;  &gt;     &lt;/li&gt; &lt;/ul&gt;  |[m
 | &lt;strong&gt;Serving cluster(s)&lt;/strong&gt; | &lt;a name="tier-serving-clusters"&gt;&lt;/a&gt; &lt;strong&gt;A dedicated cluster(s)&lt;/strong&gt; for serving queries, including &lt;a href="/concepts/indexes/" &gt;indexes&lt;/a&gt; on the materialized views. Indexes are local to the cluster in which they are created. |[m
 [m
 &lt;p&gt;Benefits of a three-tier architecture include:&lt;/p&gt;[m

[35m@@ -1,4 +1,7 @@[m
 # Appendix: External secret stores[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m
 Materialize does not directly integrate with external secret stores, but it's possible to manage this integration via Terraform.[m
 [m
 The [secret stores demo](https://github.com/MaterializeInc/demos/tree/main/integrations/terraform/secret-stores) shows how to handle [secrets](/sql/create-secret) and sensitive data with some popular secret stores. By utilizing Terraform's infrastructure-as-code model, you can automate and simplify both the initial setup and ongoing management of secret stores with Materialize.[m

 with the &#96;resource&#96; block in your Terraform configuration file.[m
[33mdiff --git a/doc/user/static/markdown-docs/security/appendix/appendix-built-in-roles/index.md b/doc/user/static/markdown-docs/security/appendix/appendix-built-in-roles/index.md[m
