 [m
 See also [Show roles in[m
 system](/security/cloud/access-control/manage-roles/#show-roles-in-system) and [Drop[m
[35m@@ -169,7 +205,7 @@[m [minformation.[m
 In Materialize, role-based access control (RBAC) governs access to **database[m
 objects** through privileges granted to database roles.[m
 [m
[31m-&gt; **Disambiguation:** Materialize uses roles to manage access control at two levels: - [Organization roles](/security/cloud/users-service-accounts/#organization-roles), which determines the access to the Console's administrative features and sets the **initial database roles** for the user/service account. - [Database roles](/security/cloud/access-control/#role-based-access-control-rbac), which controls access to database objects and operations within Materialize. The focus of this page is on managing database roles. For information on organization roles, see [Users and service accounts](/security/cloud/users-service-accounts/).[m
[32m+[m[32m&gt; **Disambiguation:** The focus of this page is on managing database roles. For information on organization roles, see [Users and service accounts](/security/cloud/users-service-accounts/).[m
 [m
 [m
 ## Required privileges for managing roles[m
[35m@@ -178,38 +214,63 @@[m [mobjects** through privileges granted to database roles.[m
 &gt; admins**](/security/cloud/users-service-accounts/#organization-roles) can manage[m
 &gt; roles (including overriding ownership requirements when granting privileges on[m
 &gt; various objects).[m
[31m-[m
[31m-[m
[31m-[m
[31m-| Role management operations | Required privileges |[m
[31m-| --- | --- |[m
[31m-| To create/revoke/grant roles | &lt;ul&gt; &lt;li&gt;&lt;code&gt;CREATEROLE&lt;/code&gt; privileges on the system. &gt; **Warning:** Roles with the &#96;CREATEROLE&#96; privilege can obtain the privileges of any other &gt; role in the system by granting themselves that role. Avoid granting &gt; &#96;CREATEROLE&#96; unnecessarily. &lt;/li&gt; &lt;/ul&gt;  |[m
[31m-| To view privileges for a role | None |[m
[31m-| To grant/revoke role privileges | &lt;ul&gt; &lt;li&gt;Ownership of affected objects.&lt;/li&gt; &lt;li&gt;&lt;code&gt;USAGE&lt;/code&gt; privileges on the containing database if the affected object is a schema.&lt;/li&gt; &lt;li&gt;&lt;code&gt;USAGE&lt;/code&gt; privileges on the containing schema if the affected object is namespaced by a schema.&lt;/li&gt; &lt;li&gt;&lt;em&gt;superuser&lt;/em&gt; status if the privilege is a system privilege.&lt;/li&gt; &lt;/ul&gt;  |[m
[31m-| To alter default privileges | &lt;ul&gt; &lt;li&gt;Role membership in &lt;code&gt;role_name&lt;/code&gt;.&lt;/li&gt; &lt;li&gt;&lt;code&gt;USAGE&lt;/code&gt; privileges on the containing database if &lt;code&gt;database_name&lt;/code&gt; is specified.&lt;/li&gt; &lt;li&gt;&lt;code&gt;USAGE&lt;/code&gt; privileges on the containing schema if &lt;code&gt;schema_name&lt;/code&gt; is specified.&lt;/li&gt; &lt;li&gt;&lt;em&gt;superuser&lt;/em&gt; status if the &lt;em&gt;target_role&lt;/em&gt; is &lt;code&gt;PUBLIC&lt;/code&gt; or &lt;strong&gt;ALL ROLES&lt;/strong&gt; is specified.&lt;/li&gt; &lt;/ul&gt;  |[m
[31m-[m
[32m+[m[32m&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&gt;[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m| Role management operations          | Required privileges      |[m
[32m+[m[32m| ----------------------------------- | ------------------------ |[m
[32m+[m[32m| To create/revoke/grant roles        | &lt;ul&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;li&gt;&lt;code&gt;CREATEROLE&lt;/code&gt; privileges on the system.&lt;/li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/ul&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m &gt; **Warning:** Roles with the &lt;code&gt;CREATEROLE&lt;/code&gt; privilege can obtain the privileges of any other[m
[32m+[m[32m&gt; role in the system by granting themselves that role. Avoid granting[m
[32m+[m[32m&gt; &lt;code&gt;CREATEROLE&lt;/code&gt; unnecessarily.[m
[32m+[m[32m&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m |[m
[32m+[m[32m| To view privileges for a role       | None                      |[m
[32m+[m[32m| To grant/revoke role privileges     | &lt;ul&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;li&gt;Ownership of affected objects.&lt;/li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;li&gt;&lt;code&gt;USAGE&lt;/code&gt; privileges on the containing database if the affected object is a schema.&lt;/li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;li&gt;&lt;code&gt;USAGE&lt;/code&gt; privileges on the containing schema if the affected object is namespaced by a schema.&lt;/li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;li&gt;&lt;em&gt;superuser&lt;/em&gt; status if the privilege is a system privilege.&lt;/li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/ul&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m|[m
[32m+[m[32m| To alter default privileges         | &lt;ul&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;li&gt;Role membership in &lt;code&gt;role_name&lt;/code&gt;.&lt;/li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;li&gt;&lt;code&gt;USAGE&lt;/code&gt; privileges on the containing database if &lt;code&gt;database_name&lt;/code&gt; is specified.&lt;/li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;li&gt;&lt;code&gt;USAGE&lt;/code&gt; privileges on the containing schema if &lt;code&gt;schema_name&lt;/code&gt; is specified.&lt;/li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;li&gt;&lt;em&gt;superuser&lt;/em&gt; status if the &lt;em&gt;target_role&lt;/em&gt; is &lt;code&gt;PUBLIC&lt;/code&gt; or &lt;strong&gt;ALL ROLES&lt;/strong&gt; is[m
[32m+[m[32mspecified.&lt;/li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/ul&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m |[m
 [m
 See also [Appendix: Privileges by[m
 command](/security/appendix/appendix-command-privileges/)[m
 [m
 ## Create a role[m
 [m
[31m-In Materialize, a database role is created:[m
[31m-- Automatically when a user/service account is created:[m
[31m-  - When a [user account is[m
[31m-  created](/security/cloud/users-service-accounts/invite-users/), an associated[m
[31m-  database role with the user email as its name is created.[m
[31m-  - When a [service account is[m
[31m-  created](/security/cloud/users-service-accounts/create-service-accounts/), an[m
[31m-  associated database role with the service account user as its name is created.[m
[31m-- Manually to create a role independent of any specific account,[m
[31m-  usually to define a set of shared privileges that can be granted to other[m
[31m-  user/service/standalone roles.[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;p&gt;In Materialize, a database role is created:&lt;/p&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;ul&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;li&gt;Automatically when a user/service account is created:[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;ul&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;li&gt;When a &lt;a href="/security/cloud/users-service-accounts/invite-users/" &gt;user account is[m
[32m+[m[32mcreated&lt;/a&gt;, an associated[m
[32m+[m[32mdatabase role with the user email as its name is created.&lt;/li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;li&gt;When a &lt;a href="/security/cloud/users-service-accounts/create-service-accounts/" &gt;service account is[m
[32m+[m[32mcreated&lt;/a&gt;, an[m
[32m+[m[32massociated database role with the service account user as its name is created.&lt;/li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/ul&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;li&gt;Manually to create a role independent of any specific account,[m
[32m+[m[32musually to define a set of shared privileges that can be granted to other[m
[32m+[m[32muser/service/standalone roles.&lt;/li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/ul&gt;[m
 [m
 To create a new role manually, use the [&#96;CREATE ROLE&#96;](/sql/create-role/)[m
 statement.[m
 [m
[31m-&gt; **Privilege(s) required to run the command:** - &#96;CREATEROLE&#96; privileges on the system.[m
[32m+[m[32m&gt; **Privilege(s) required to run the command:** &lt;ul&gt; &lt;li&gt;&lt;code&gt;CREATEROLE&lt;/code&gt; privileges on the system.&lt;/li&gt; &lt;/ul&gt;[m
 [m
 [m
 &#96;&#96;&#96;mzsql[m
[35m@@ -217,8 +278,9 @@[m [mCREATE ROLE &lt;role_name&gt; [WITH INHERIT];[m
 -- WITH INHERIT behavior is implied and does not need to be specified.[m
 &#96;&#96;&#96;[m
 [m
[31m-&gt; **Tip:** Role names cannot start with &#96;mz_&#96; and &#96;pg_&#96; as they are reserved for system[m
[32m+[m[32m&gt; **Tip:** Role names cannot start with &lt;code&gt;mz_&lt;/code&gt; and &lt;code&gt;pg_&lt;/code&gt; as they are reserved for system[m
 &gt; roles.[m
[32m+[m[32m&gt;[m
 [m
 [m
 For example, the following creates:[m
[35m@@ -233,6 +295,8 @@[m [mFor example, the following creates:[m
 Create a role for users who need to perform compute/transform operations in[m
 the compute/transform cluster(s). This role will handle creating views,[m
 materialized views, and other transformation objects.[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m
 &#96;&#96;&#96;mzsql[m
 CREATE ROLE view_manager;[m
 [m
[35m@@ -242,6 +306,8 @@[m [mCREATE ROLE view_manager;[m
 [m
 Create a role for users who need to manage indexes on the serving[m
 cluster(s). This role will handle creating indexes to serve results.[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m
 &#96;&#96;&#96;mzsql[m
 CREATE ROLE serving_index_manager;[m
 [m
[35m@@ -250,6 +316,8 @@[m [mCREATE ROLE serving_index_manager;[m
 **Data reader role:**[m
 [m
 Create a role for users who need to read results from the serving cluster.[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m
 &#96;&#96;&#96;mzsql[m
 CREATE ROLE data_reader;[m
 [m
[35m@@ -261,27 +329,34 @@[m [mIn Materialize, a role is created with inheritance support. With inheritance,[m
 when a role is granted to another role (i.e., the target role), the target role[m
 inherits privileges (not role attributes and parameters) through the other role.[m
 All roles in Materialize are automatically members of[m
[31m-[&#96;PUBLIC&#96;](/security/appendix/appendix-built-in-roles/#public-role). As[m
[31m-such, every role includes inherited privileges from &#96;PUBLIC&#96;.[m
[31m-[m
[31m-Once a role is created, you can:[m
[31m-[m
[31m-- [Manage its current[m
[31m-  privileges](/security/cloud/access-control/manage-roles/#manage-current-privileges-for-a-role)[m
[31m-  (i.e., privileges on existing objects):[m
[31m-  - By granting privileges for a role or revoking privileges from a role.[m
[31m-  - By granting other roles to the role or revoking roles from the role.[m
[31m-    *Recommended for user account/service account roles.*[m
[31m-- [Manage its future[m
[31m-  privileges](/security/cloud/access-control/manage-roles/#manage-future-privileges-for-a-role)[m
[31m-  (i.e., privileges on objects created in the future):[m
[31m-  - By defining default privileges for objects. With default privileges in[m
[31m-   place, a role is automatically granted/revoked privileges as new objects are[m
[31m-   created by **others** (When an object is created, the creator is granted all[m
[31m-   [applicable privileges](/security/appendix/appendix-privileges/) for that[m
[31m-   object automatically).[m
[31m-[m
[31m-&gt; **Disambiguation:** - Use &#96;GRANT|REVOKE ...&#96; to modify privileges on **existing** objects. - Use &#96;ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES&#96; to ensure that privileges are automatically granted or revoked when **new objects** of a certain type are created by others. Then, as needed, you can use &#96;GRANT|REVOKE &lt;privilege&gt;&#96; to adjust those privileges.[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;a href="/security/appendix/appendix-built-in-roles/#public-role" &gt;&lt;code&gt;PUBLIC&lt;/code&gt;&lt;/a&gt;. As[m
[32m+[m[32msuch, every role includes inherited privileges from &lt;code&gt;PUBLIC&lt;/code&gt;.[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;p&gt;Once a role is created, you can:&lt;/p&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;ul&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;li&gt;&lt;a href="/security/cloud/access-control/manage-roles/#manage-current-privileges-for-a-role" &gt;Manage its current[m
[32m+[m[32mprivileges&lt;/a&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m(i.e., privileges on existing objects):[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;ul&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;li&gt;By granting privileges for a role or revoking privileges from a role.&lt;/li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;li&gt;By granting other roles to the role or revoking roles from the role.[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;em&gt;Recommended for user account/service account roles.&lt;/em&gt;&lt;/li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/ul&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;li&gt;&lt;a href="/security/cloud/access-control/manage-roles/#manage-future-privileges-for-a-role" &gt;Manage its future[m
[32m+[m[32mprivileges&lt;/a&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m(i.e., privileges on objects created in the future):[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;ul&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;li&gt;By defining default privileges for objects. With default privileges in[m
[32m+[m[32mplace, a role is automatically granted/revoked privileges as new objects are[m
[32m+[m[32mcreated by &lt;strong&gt;others&lt;/strong&gt; (When an object is created, the creator is granted all[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;a href="/security/appendix/appendix-privileges/" &gt;applicable privileges&lt;/a&gt; for that[m
[32m+[m[32mobject automatically).&lt;/li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/ul&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&lt;/ul&gt;[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m&gt; **Disambiguation:** &lt;ul&gt; &lt;li&gt; &lt;p&gt;Use &lt;code&gt;GRANT|REVOKE ...&lt;/code&gt; to modify privileges on &lt;strong&gt;existing&lt;/strong&gt; objects.&lt;/p&gt; &lt;/li&gt; &lt;li&gt; &lt;p&gt;Use &lt;code&gt;ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES&lt;/code&gt; to ensure that privileges are automatically granted or revoked when &lt;strong&gt;new objects&lt;/strong&gt; of a certain type are created by others. Then, as needed, you can use &lt;code&gt;GRANT|REVOKE &amp;lt;privilege&amp;gt;&lt;/code&gt; to adjust those privileges.&lt;/p&gt; &lt;/li&gt; &lt;/ul&gt;[m
 [m
 [m
 See also:[m
[35m@@ -298,7 +373,9 @@[m [mThe examples below assume:[m
 - The existence of a &#96;source_cluster&#96;, a &#96;compute_cluster&#96;, and a[m
   &#96;serving_cluster&#96;. For example:[m
 [m
[31m-  &lt;no value&gt;&#96;&#96;&#96;mzsql[m
[32m+[m[32m  &lt;no value&gt;[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m  &#96;&#96;&#96;mzsql[m

   CREATE CLUSTER compute_cluster (SIZE = '25cc');[m
   CREATE CLUSTER serving_cluster (SIZE = '25cc');[m
[35m@@ -308,7 +385,9 @@[m [mThe examples below assume:[m

   database. For example:[m
 [m

[32m+[m[32m  &lt;no value&gt;[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m  &#96;&#96;&#96;mzsql[m
   CREATE DATABASE IF NOT EXISTS mydb;[m
   CREATE SCHEMA IF NOT EXISTS mydb.sales;[m
 [m
[35m@@ -317,7 +396,9 @@[m [mThe examples below assume:[m
 - The existence of &#96;items&#96;, &#96;orders&#96;, and &#96;sales_items&#96; tables within the[m
   &#96;mydb.sales&#96; schema. For example:[m
 [m
[31m-  &lt;no value&gt;&#96;&#96;&#96;mzsql[m
[32m+[m[32m  &lt;no value&gt;[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m  &#96;&#96;&#96;mzsql[m
   SET CLUSTER = source_cluster;[m
 [m
   SET DATABASE = mydb;[m

 &#96;&#96;&#96;[m

[31m-&gt; such, every role includes inherited privileges from &#96;PUBLIC&#96;.[m
[32m+[m[32m&gt; &lt;a href="/security/appendix/appendix-built-in-roles/#public-role" &gt;&lt;code&gt;PUBLIC&lt;/code&gt;&lt;/a&gt;. As[m
[32m+[m[32m&gt; such, every role includes inherited privileges from &lt;code&gt;PUBLIC&lt;/code&gt;.[m
[32m+[m[32m&gt;[m
 [m
 [m
 For example:[m
[35m@@ -396,6 +478,8 @@[m [mTo view privileges for a[m
 PRIVILEGES&#96;](/sql/show-privileges) on the role named after the user's email[m
 (automatically created when the account is activated; i.e., first time the[m

 [m

[32m+[m
 &#96;&#96;&#96;mzsql[m
 SHOW PRIVILEGES FOR view_manager;[m
 [m

 &gt; return fields; e.g., &#96;SHOW PRIVILEGES FOR view_manager WHERE[m
 &gt; name='quickstart';&#96;.[m

 [m
 ### Grant privileges to a role[m
[35m@@ -526,7 +620,7 @@[m [mTo grant [privileges](/security/appendix/appendix-command-privileges/) to[m
 a role, use the [&#96;GRANT PRIVILEGE&#96;](/sql/grant-privilege/) statement (see[m
 [&#96;GRANT PRIVILEGE&#96;](/sql/grant-privilege/) for the full syntax)[m
 [m
[31m-&gt; **Privilege(s) required to run the command:** - Ownership of affected objects. - &#96;USAGE&#96; privileges on the containing database if the affected object is a schema. - &#96;USAGE&#96; privileges on the containing schema if the affected object is namespaced by a schema. - _superuser_ status if the privilege is a system privilege. To override the **object ownership** requirements to grant privileges, run as an Organization admin.[m
[32m+[m[32m&gt; **Privilege(s) required to run the command:** &lt;ul&gt; &lt;li&gt;Ownership of affected objects.&lt;/li&gt; &lt;li&gt;&lt;code&gt;USAGE&lt;/code&gt; privileges on the containing database if the affected object is a schema.&lt;/li&gt; &lt;li&gt;&lt;code&gt;USAGE&lt;/code&gt; privileges on the containing schema if the affected object is namespaced by a schema.&lt;/li&gt; &lt;li&gt;&lt;em&gt;superuser&lt;/em&gt; status if the privilege is a system privilege.&lt;/li&gt; &lt;/ul&gt; To override the **object ownership** requirements to grant privileges, run as an Organization admin.[m
 [m
 [m
 &#96;&#96;&#96;mzsql[m

 [m
 When possible, avoid granting privileges directly to individual user or service[m
 account roles (which are named after email addresses or service account user).[m
[31m-Instead, create reusable, functional roles (e.g., &#96;data_reader&#96;, &#96;view_manager&#96;)[m
[32m+[m[32mInstead, create reusable, functional roles (e.g., &lt;code&gt;data_reader&lt;/code&gt;, &lt;code&gt;view_manager&lt;/code&gt;)[m
 with well-defined privileges, and grant these roles to the individual user or[m
 service account roles. You can also grant functional roles to other functional[m
 roles to compose more complex functional roles.[m

[31m-&gt;   replicas, sources, sinks), access is also required for the associated cluster.[m
[31m-&gt; - For objects in a schema, access is also required for the schema.[m

[31m-&gt; by command](/security/appendix/appendix-command-privileges/).[m
[32m+[m[32m&gt; **Note:** &lt;p&gt;Various SQL operations require additional privileges on related objects, such[m
[32m+[m[32m&gt; as:&lt;/p&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&gt; &lt;ul&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&gt; &lt;li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&gt; &lt;p&gt;For objects that use compute resources (e.g., indexes, materialized views,[m
[32m+[m[32m&gt; replicas, sources, sinks), access is also required for the associated cluster.&lt;/p&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&gt; &lt;/li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&gt; &lt;li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&gt; &lt;p&gt;For objects in a schema, access is also required for the schema.&lt;/p&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&gt; &lt;/li&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&gt; &lt;/ul&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&gt; &lt;p&gt;For details on SQL operations and needed privileges, see &lt;a href="/security/appendix/appendix-command-privileges/" &gt;Appendix: Privileges[m
[32m+[m[32m&gt; by command&lt;/a&gt;.&lt;/p&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&gt;[m

 &#96;&#96;&#96;mzsql[m

 &#96;&#96;&#96;[m

 &#96;&#96;&#96;mzsql[m

 [m
 {{&lt; note &gt;}}[m
 If a query directly references a view or materialized view:[m

 {{&lt;/ note &gt;}}[m

[32m+[m
 &#96;&#96;&#96;mzsql[m
 -- To select from **existing** views/materialized views/tables/sources:[m
 -- Need USAGE on schema and cluster[m

 [m
 [m
 Review the privileges granted to the &#96;data_reader&#96; role:[m

 &#96;&#96;&#96;mzsql[m
 SHOW PRIVILEGES FOR data_reader;[m
 [m
[35m@@ -778,17 +892,19 @@[m [mOnce a role is created, you can modify its privileges either:[m
 [m
 &gt; **Tip:** When possible, avoid granting privileges directly to individual user or service[m
 &gt; account roles (which are named after email addresses or service account user).[m
[31m-&gt; Instead, create reusable, functional roles (e.g., &#96;data_reader&#96;, &#96;view_manager&#96;)[m
[32m+[m[32m&gt; Instead, create reusable, functional roles (e.g., &lt;code&gt;data_reader&lt;/code&gt;, &lt;code&gt;view_manager&lt;/code&gt;)[m
 &gt; with well-defined privileges, and grant these roles to the individual user or[m
 &gt; service account roles. You can also grant functional roles to other functional[m
 &gt; roles to compose more complex functional roles.[m
[32m+[m[32m&gt;[m
[32m+[m[32m&gt;[m
 [m
 [m
 To grant a role to another role (where the role can be a user role/service[m
 account role/functional role), use the [&#96;GRANT ROLE&#96;](/sql/grant-role/)[m
 statement (see [&#96;GRANT ROLE&#96;](/sql/grant-role/) for full syntax):[m
 [m
[31m-&gt; **Privilege(s) required to run the command:** - &#96;CREATEROLE&#96; privileges on the system. Organization admin has the required privileges on the system.[m
[32m+[m[32m&gt; **Privilege(s) required to run the command:** &lt;ul&gt; &lt;li&gt;&lt;code&gt;CREATEROLE&lt;/code&gt; privileges on the system.&lt;/li&gt; &lt;/ul&gt; Organization admin has the required privileges on the system.[m
 [m
 [m
 &#96;&#96;&#96;mzsql[m
[35m@@ -813,6 +929,8 @@[m [mIn the following examples,[m
 [m
 The following grants the &#96;view_manager&#96; role to the role associated with the[m
 user &#96;blue.berry@example.com&#96;.[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m
 &#96;&#96;&#96;mzsql[m
 GRANT view_manager TO "blue.berry@example.com";[m
 [m
[35m@@ -820,6 +938,8 @@[m [mGRANT view_manager TO "blue.berry@example.com";[m
 [m
 [m
 Review the privileges granted to the &#96;blue.berry@example.com&#96; role:[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m
 &#96;&#96;&#96;mzsql[m
 SHOW PRIVILEGES FOR "blue.berry@example.com";[m
 [m
[35m@@ -850,6 +970,8 @@[m [mmz_system         | PUBLIC       |             |        | quickstart        | cl[m

 &#96;blue.berry@example.com&#96; can create objects in the &#96;mydb.sales&#96; schema on[m
 the &#96;compute_cluster&#96;.[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m
 &#96;&#96;&#96;mzsql[m
 -- run as blue.berry@example.com[m
 SET CLUSTER TO compute_cluster;[m
